channel.title: TechNode
channel.description: Latest news and trends about tech in China

item.title: Tencent responds to OpenClaw SkillHub dispute
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Tencent has responded after a debate emerged on X around SkillHub, a collection of skills launched by the company for the OpenClaw ecosystem. The discussion followed remarks from Peter Steinberger, creator of the OpenClaw AI agent framework, who said heavy automated requests targeting ClawHub, an MIT-licensed skill directory for OpenClaw, had increased his server costs. […]
item.content:encoded:
Tencent has responded after a debate emerged on X around SkillHub, a collection of skills launched by the company for the OpenClaw ecosystem. The discussion followed remarks from Peter Steinberger, creator of the OpenClaw AI agent framework, who said heavy automated requests targeting ClawHub, an MIT-licensed skill directory for OpenClaw, had increased his server costs.

Tencent AI later replied in the thread that SkillHub functions as a localized mirror aimed at improving access for users in China and that the platform consistently credits ClawHub as the original source. The company said that during SkillHub’s first week online, it served about 180GB of content while pulling only around 1GB of data from the official source through non-concurrent requests. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: AWE 2026: Dreame Technology displays Nebula Next 01X concept SUV
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: At the Appliance & Electronics World Expo (AWE) 2026, Dreame Technology’s automotive brand Starry Sky Project showcased its first SUV concept car, the Nebula Next 01X. The vehicle adopts a design language inspired by supercars, featuring a front fascia centered on a multifunctional aerodynamic structure and L-shaped lighting elements. The body uses lightweight materials such […]
item.content:encoded:
At the Appliance & Electronics World Expo (AWE) 2026, Dreame Technology’s automotive brand Starry Sky Project showcased its first SUV concept car, the Nebula Next 01X. The vehicle adopts a design language inspired by supercars, featuring a front fascia centered on a multifunctional aerodynamic structure and L-shaped lighting elements. The body uses lightweight materials such as carbon fiber and adopts a fastback-style profile.

For the chassis, the vehicle is equipped with the Starry Sky Tianxun intelligent chassis system, which supports functions such as lateral parking and in-place turning. In terms of power, the concept car is fitted with a 60Ah all-solid-state battery. The company says the battery has a single-cell energy density exceeding 450Wh/kg and could potentially reach 800Wh/kg in the future. According to the plan, the model is expected to enter mass production in 2027. [TechNode Reporting]

item.title: AWE 2026: Tesla showcases third-generation humanoid robot
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Today, the Appliance & Electronics World Expo (AWE) 2026 opened in Shanghai. Tesla showcased the Tesla Cybertruck and a humanoid robot, with its upcoming third-generation humanoid model drawing particular attention. The robot is Tesla’s first version designed for large-scale mass production, with production planned to begin by the end of 2026 and a long-term capacity […]
item.content:encoded:
Today, the Appliance & Electronics World Expo (AWE) 2026 opened in Shanghai. Tesla showcased the Tesla Cybertruck and a humanoid robot, with its upcoming third-generation humanoid model drawing particular attention. The robot is Tesla’s first version designed for large-scale mass production, with production planned to begin by the end of 2026 and a long-term capacity target of up to one million units. The company expects the cost per unit to be kept below $20,000.

The robot leverages Tesla’s vision-based neural network technology developed for autonomous driving, enabling it to perform complex tasks and learn new skills by observing human behavior. In the future, it could help free humans from dangerous, repetitive, and tedious work. [TechNode Reporting]

item.title: China’s Super Computing Network offers 10 million free tokens per user in OpenClaw promotion
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: China’s Super Computing Network has launched a two-week OpenClaw user benefits campaign, offering 10 million tokens per user for free to all users during the period. The initiative aims to lower the barrier to using AI agents and ease the pressure on individual developers and small businesses caused by rising computing costs. Users can log […]
item.content:encoded:
China’s Super Computing Network has launched a two-week OpenClaw user benefits campaign, offering 10 million tokens per user for free to all users during the period. The initiative aims to lower the barrier to using AI agents and ease the pressure on individual developers and small businesses caused by rising computing costs. Users can log in to the Super Computing Network’s official website via PC and deploy OpenClaw with one click in the chat interface to automatically claim the benefit.

The platform also announced a renewal price as low as 0.1 yuan ($0.01) per million tokens, a significant discount compared with the market average. The giveaway is expected to help accelerate the adoption of AI applications and promote the expansion of agent technologies into a broader range of scenarios. [TechNode Reporting]

item.title: Baidu launches zero-deployment OpenClaw service DuClaw
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Baidu’s cloud unit, Baidu AI Cloud, has launched DuClaw, a zero-deployment OpenClaw service aimed at users without technical setup experience, according to official information from the company. The web-based product does not require server deployment, image selection, or API key configuration, and is designed for direct use through a browser. Baidu AI Cloud said DuClaw […]
item.content:encoded:
Baidu’s cloud unit, Baidu AI Cloud, has launched DuClaw, a zero-deployment OpenClaw service aimed at users without technical setup experience, according to official information from the company. The web-based product does not require server deployment, image selection, or API key configuration, and is designed for direct use through a browser.

Baidu AI Cloud said DuClaw includes built-in access to services such as Baidu Search, Baidu Baike, and Baidu Scholar, and supports model switching across options including DeepSeek, Kimi-K2.5, GLM-5, and MiniMax-M2.5. The company added that the product is currently available on the web and is planned to connect with mainstream messaging apps later. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: BYD weighs entry into Formula One and endurance racing, report says
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: BYD is examining options to enter global motorsport, including Formula One and endurance racing, as it looks to raise its brand profile overseas, according to people familiar with the matter. The Chinese automaker is said to be considering multiple routes, ranging from the World Endurance Championship to F1, including building its own team or pursuing […]
item.content:encoded:
BYD is examining options to enter global motorsport, including Formula One and endurance racing, as it looks to raise its brand profile overseas, according to people familiar with the matter. The Chinese automaker is said to be considering multiple routes, ranging from the World Endurance Championship to F1, including building its own team or pursuing an acquisition.

No decision has been made and BYD may ultimately choose not to enter any series. The report said the cost of joining F1 could be a major hurdle, with a single season potentially requiring as much as $500 million. BYD did not respond to a request for comment. [Bloomberg]

item.title: Tencent is said to be developing a top-secret AI agent project for WeChat
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Tencent is secretly developing an AI agent for its WeChat app, according to The Information. The project is reportedly classified internally as top secret and is designed to connect with WeChat’s mini program ecosystem, allowing users to complete tasks such as ride-hailing and food delivery through AI-driven actions. Tencent is said to be targeting a […]
item.content:encoded: Tencent is secretly developing an AI agent for its WeChat app, according to The Information. The project is reportedly classified internally as top secret and is designed to connect with WeChat’s mini program ecosystem, allowing users to complete tasks such as ride-hailing and food delivery through AI-driven actions. Tencent is said to be targeting a gray-box test around midyear, followed by a broader rollout in the third quarter if the feature is ready. [The Information]

item.title: China cyber emergency center flags security risks in AI agent OpenClaw
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: The National Computer Network Emergency Response Technical Team/Coordination Center of China on Tuesday issued a risk alert regarding the secure use of the AI agent software OpenClaw. The program has recently gained popularity for enabling users to control computers through natural language commands. However, the center warned that its default security configurations are relatively weak. […]
item.content:encoded:
The National Computer Network Emergency Response Technical Team/Coordination Center of China on Tuesday issued a risk alert regarding the secure use of the AI agent software OpenClaw. The program has recently gained popularity for enabling users to control computers through natural language commands. However, the center warned that its default security configurations are relatively weak. When granted excessive system privileges, the software may pose significant security risks.

Attackers could exploit vulnerabilities to gain control of the system, potentially leading to credential leakage, accidental deletion of data, or malicious plugin poisoning. Several medium- to high-severity vulnerabilities have already been disclosed, posing threats to both personal privacy and enterprises’ core data security.

The agency advised users to strengthen network isolation, improve credential management, strictly review the sources of plugins, and apply security patches in a timely manner to mitigate potential threats. [CNCERT, in Chinese]

item.title: Xiaohongshu targets AI-managed accounts in push to clean up content
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Chinese social platform Xiaohongshu said in a statement on Tuesday that it will strictly crack down on AI-managed accounts in order to maintain the authenticity of its community ecosystem. The platform has explicitly banned the use of technical tools to simulate human creators for producing non-authentic content or conducting fake interactions. It will focus on […]
item.content:encoded:
Chinese social platform Xiaohongshu said in a statement on Tuesday that it will strictly crack down on AI-managed accounts in order to maintain the authenticity of its community ecosystem. The platform has explicitly banned the use of technical tools to simulate human creators for producing non-authentic content or conducting fake interactions. It will focus on addressing violations such as AI-generated low-quality content at scale, fake product endorsements, account trading, and attempts to bypass platform detection.

For ordinary accounts with occasional violations, the platform will issue warnings or traffic restrictions, while accounts fully operated by AI will be permanently banned. The campaign aims to curb the spread of so-called digital garbage content, protect user rights, and encourage users to participate in reporting suspicious activity. [TechNode Reporting]

item.title: OpenClaw sparks boom as Chinese firms race into the AI agent era
item.dc:creator: Jessie Wu
item.description: The open-source AI agent framework OpenClaw has recently gone viral worldwide, drawing significant attention from the tech industry. By enabling AI to move beyond generating content to actually executing tasks, the framework is widely seen as a key step toward the AI agent era. A growing number of Chinese technology companies are actively exploring similar […]
item.content:encoded:
The open-source AI agent framework OpenClaw has recently gone viral worldwide, drawing significant attention from the tech industry. By enabling AI to move beyond generating content to actually executing tasks, the framework is widely seen as a key step toward the AI agent era. A growing number of Chinese technology companies are actively exploring similar approaches and rolling out related products.

Moonshot AI was among the first to launch Kimi Claw, a native integration with OpenClaw. The product emphasizes zero-code deployment and one-click setup, while also offering free computing power subsidies for OpenClaw calls, lowering the barrier for users. The move has attracted a large influx of users and helped accelerate the company’s overseas expansion, with the number of paying international users surging and overseas revenue surpassing domestic revenue for the first time.

MiniMax has also joined the trend, launching MaxClaw, a cloud-based AI assistant built on OpenClaw. The product aims to provide users with a cloud-hosted environment for running AI agents, focusing on performance and ease of use.

Zhipu AI has taken a different approach. It collaborated with Alibaba Cloud’s AgentBay to launch AutoGLM–OpenClaw, a cloud-based version built on an OpenClaw image. The product allows companies to deploy and run AI agents in the cloud, reducing the need for local infrastructure.

Meanwhile, Tencent has also moved into the space with WorkBuddy, an AI-powered workplace assistant. The tool integrates with the company’s cloud and AI ecosystem and supports compatibility with OpenClaw skills. Users can access it through WeCom or a web-based interface, enabling remote access to AI-assisted office functions without requiring complex local deployment.

WorkBuddy focuses on the workplace and emphasizes task execution capabilities. It can automatically handle emails by identifying content, organizing messages into categories, and replying to common emails based on preset rules.

For meeting scheduling, it can coordinate participants’ availability and automatically generate and send meeting invitations. When it comes to document management, the system can extract key information and produce concise summaries.

China’s rapid push into the OpenClaw ecosystem could have broad implications for the AI industry. It reflects a shift from traditional AI models toward AI agents capable of breaking down tasks, searching the web, and calling external tools, turning large models into more practical productivity systems. This evolution could accelerate digital transformation across industries.

Frequent task execution increases token consumption and creates new revenue streams for model providers. At the same time, cloud vendors are exploring service models built around AI agents. These efforts could lead to the formation of a new commercial ecosystem.

However, the rapid rise of AI agents also brings challenges. Wider deployment raises concerns around data security and privacy protection, as systems gain access to more user data and enterprise workflows. Increased automation may also reshape parts of the labor market as some routine tasks become machine-executable.
